description_long: This is a rustic, round-bodied clay jug with a single, thick handle attached near the neck and shoulder, sitting on a wooden plank surface. The jug has a wide, bulbous body that tapers smoothly to a short, narrow neck with a pronounced, outward-flaring rim. The surface of the jug is matte and unglazed, showing a natural, earthy brown color with subtle speckling and rough texture, characteristic of hand-thrown pottery. The handle is robust and curves from the upper neck to the shoulder, providing a secure grip for lifting or pouring. There are no visible decorative markings, bands, or reinforcements, giving the jug a utilitarian, traditional appearance. The opening at the top is uncovered, indicating the absence of a lid.
description: A large, round, unglazed clay jug with a single sturdy handle and a wide mouth, featuring a rough, earthy brown surface and no visible markings.
description_view_0: The jug is seen from the front, showing the handle on the right and the wide, flared rim clearly visible.
description_view_1: The jug is viewed from the back, with the handle centered and the body’s curvature prominent.
description_view_2: The jug is seen from the side, highlighting the handle’s attachment and the smooth, rounded body.
description_view_3: The jug is viewed from the opposite side of the handle, showing the uninterrupted curve of the body and neck.
